transcript.text: Not too bad.
How's the weather up in Boston?
Very, very humid.
Oh.
It's not been a nice summer so far.
I think I mentioned last time, this is like, we probably get like, I'd say eight weeks of good weather a year.
Yeah.
And this is usually when we get it, and so now we're not looking good.
Oh, man.
But, you know, you deal.
Yeah.
Hey, Luke.
Hello.
I didn't know you.
Sorry, go ahead.
Yeah, it's fine.
I didn't know you went in the office on one stage.
Sorry?
Sorry, I was talking to Luke.
Oh, Luke, okay.
I do now.
My wife picked me up because I'm too depressing around the house during the week.
I
Hey, Victoria.
Hello.
All right.
I think Mara might join a little late, so we can get started.
So quick performance update as usual.
So for our blog pages, we saw a bit of a rebound in terms of sessions and views.
It's kind of worse the decline we've seen over the past, over the last week, actually.
So we're kind of back in stable territory again over there.

For the per diem pages, again, not major changes the past couple of weeks, obviously we've seen sessions increase, but it's pretty much holding pattern over here, just swapping the odd position here and there.
We're not seeing the of the major.
So the expense category changes.
The decline sort of continues.
It's not as steep as it was, obviously, during the drop-off, but over the past couple months, as you can see from the sessions trend, we have been bleeding some sessions a bit.
We've lost about 30% compared to two months ago, and the sort of frustrating thing is the page-level session data is not quite squaring up with the overall loss.
So we're not able to get full visibility to which pages exactly are, you know, leading to the most.
These pages over here, they're the ones that have lost at least three sessions, and then these pages below basically did not receive any traffic last week compared to two months back.
So this is about as deep as we're able to get on these pages for now.
Of course, the AI overview sort of effect is still the major theory over here, but generally we're slowly.
Keep losing sessions on these pages, and yeah, that's kind of the story with the expense category.
For the tuning pages, we lost about 10 sessions from last week.
They weren't super high to begin with, so it's not as alarming as it sounds.
Obviously, we lost a bunch of views, but generally, we recommend waiting for another week.
And over here, again, there was some weird discrepancy between GSC and GA4.
GSC is actually showing more clicks than GA4 first row sessions, so something odd going on over there, as usual, between the two products.
The second batch of tuning pages, pretty much stable, not much changed over here.
Similar story for the treasury pages as well.
And then the vendor pages, obviously, we saw a huge bump in pretty much sessions and obviously in views as well.
The sessions were spread out across all of the pages.
So these six ones over here received at least three sessions.
So generally, performance seems to be on the up with regards to vendor pages.
So yes, any questions about any of that I can answer?
All right.
I'm not too worried about the expense categories.
It's probably just AIO reviews.
We're seeing it everywhere.
I don't think there's much to do there.
It's like super informational.
And I don't think that traffic's coming back.
So I wouldn't worry about it too much.
Yeah, that was kind of what we were thinking as well.
And we still seem to be ranking for the core keywords.
So just wanted to report on like the steady decline we're seeing over there.
So moving on to the production and off side of things where I think we have a lot to discuss.
So generally, just responding to your question, Victoria in Slack, so generally for all the pages, so
Just given that we're doing the audit and that we're shifting around a lot of pieces on the board, the deadline for creation for all the pages is Friday.
So we'll have them for your review by then.
And then Mika has just provided some updates over here in terms of, you know, the DevOps versus web hosting and site building.
We updated the metadata for the previous versus pages.
So that's been done.
With regards to your question in Slack about publishing 37 pages by Wednesday, that might be a bit of a stretch for us.
Is it okay if we do it by Friday next week?
Or is there like a hard deadline for you with regards to that?
Friday's okay.
Do you think moving forward we can increase the amount of pages published per week?
And I know we're talking about production, but like publishing, is that also something?
Could increase the capacity?
Yeah, it's something we are looking at internally.
We literally had a meeting earlier today about this.
The idea is that automating it would be the best way forward because right now the manual publishing is really the sort of blocker in all of this.
Creating the pages is pretty straightforward.
So what we're doing is our dev team is a little sort of caught up in the audit this week.
But starting from next week, we will be devoting resources to really just design the publishing automation.
And then we can start testing that real quick and hopefully start automating that so that we can really increase the volume.
That will take some time to come up.
So for the next couple to three weeks, it's going to be a bit of a blocker.
We can still continue doing it at the volume at which we're doing right now.
But once the automation is in, we can then really wrap up and sort of scale.
That sounds good.
The only thing I would worry about is the QA process.
If we were to automate this, how do we ensure we're still reviewing everything?
That makes perfect sense.
I can also jump in there.
First and foremost, thank you for letting us know of the QA slip earlier.
I'm glad that the team was able to jump in and fix that immediately.
First and foremost, it should never happen.
So thank you for flagging it for us.
And we did have the meeting earlier today to discuss automating publishing.
Part of that conversation was also a QA layer as well.
So because we're building something custom for RAMP, that will be a part of the operational process.
Ideally, it would be both a human being and then also part of our...
Manual operations.
So there will be a layer there so that nothing slips through.
But also keeping in mind that it's a new process.
We can also just provide a report maybe each week.
Whatever would make us feel good about giving you visibility that there aren't any slips as we move into a new automated process.
We will make sure to do that as well.
I know that's a lot of speculation or a lot of future focus, but we will make sure to provide some type of visibility and report in.
And we're also making sure to build that layer in as well, both at a personal level and then also in the workflow that we're building for RAMP as well.
Yeah, that sounds good.
Thank you, Mara.
Yeah, of course.
And if there are any like, we fully understand how open that is.

So if you have any questions whatsoever or any other concerns, please ping me at any time and we'll be able to answer, maybe give a little bit more context to on like how the engineering team is building in that layer.
Just so that you can have a little bit more peace of mind as well.
Perfect.
Sounds good.
Cool.
So moving on to the blog refresh articles.
Matt, we addressed your comments on all of them at this point.
Obviously, we retired one of those, given the overlap that you pointed out.
And then we addressed the edits together and the comment that you had left regarding the H3s and H2s.
That one, I had responded to it as well.
So we're working on H3 for this week.
We should have it out by early on Friday.
So pretty much things are on track over there.

So, yeah, I just wanted to also just hear your thoughts on, like, the initial batch, if there's something we need to fix or if there was anything that was way off or something that was done well, et cetera, just need to hear your feedback so that we can keep improving it.
Yeah, I, um...
So, yeah, when I leave feedback on the drafts, who is actually looking at that?
Is it just the reviewer preparer for the draft, or do you get pinged on that as well?
I look at it as well, and also the person who generated the article also looks at it, and the action we take is basically we use that to give it, use that as input basically in the flows.
Got it, okay.

Yeah, I think the, I did leave one kind of like high level comment on the lower card interest rates draft, which was essentially that it does feel as though once, when I sent over those brand kit variables that we'd been using in our own workflows, it felt like the tone shift went like way over and on the conversational side.
And so some of the word choice was just, you know, not really.
And that RAMP would say.
So I don't know if there's some way to account for that at a high level.
Because I do understand, obviously, that it's kind of a very narrow line to walk, especially as somebody who's been with RAMP for a while.
And like, I know what we wouldn't say.
And so like, it's tough to kind of convey a lot of those, you know, it's probably going to come up on a case by case basis.
But in general, I think, like, yeah, we probably need to read the tone back a little bit.
Because sometimes, yeah, some of the word choices are just a little hokey.
Okay.
Was that the case on all the three articles or just that first one?
I know the first one, we were kind of trying to find a middle ground.
And the second and third, we kind of reeled back a bit.
So interesting.
Yeah, I thought the second and third, I thought, were even more drastic than the first.
Okay.
All right.
All right.
We will take a look at that and make sure it's addressed in this week's batch.
So yeah, thank you very much for that.
That's super helpful.
Yeah, no problem.

And then one other trend that I saw across all of them, all the posts, was just kind of like a few instances where it was just a wall of text, where there wasn't really any kind of differentiation in formatting.

So like trying to be a little more strategic or thoughtful about using either ordered or unordered lists, tables, those kinds of things, to kind of break up some of the just text density on the page, especially on mobile.
It'll just be the entire screen on mobile, you know what I mean, whole paragraph.
So yeah, those are the two big kind of high-level comments.
Sure.
Yeah.
Yeah, we will address those.
The, yeah, wall of text.
That's more of a page-formatic thing that we need to address, obviously, but we'll get that down, for sure.
So, yeah.
That was pretty much it for this week.
We'll obviously keep you updated on the state of the automation for the vendor pages and reach out in case of any questions or clarifications, etc.
So, yeah, that's pretty much it.
Thank you.
Thank you.
Great.
Thanks.
Thanks, everyone.
Have a good day.
See ya.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly check-in between Ramp and GrowthX to discuss performance updates, content production, and blog refresh progress.](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=1.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Blog/per diem pages stable; expense category pages declining (\~30% loss over 2 months)](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=83.0)
  - [Vendor pages saw significant session/view increases](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=226.0)
  - [Content production deadline extended to Friday; publishing automation in development](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
  - [Blog refresh articles need tone adjustment and improved formatting](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=629.0)

## Topics

### Performance Updates

  - [Blog pages: Rebounded in sessions/views, stabilizing after recent decline](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=83.0)
  - [Per diem pages: Holding steady, minor position changes](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=98.0)
  - [Expense category pages:](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=112.0)
      - [Continued decline (\~30% session loss over 2 months)](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=120.0)
      - [Difficulty pinpointing exact pages causing loss](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=132.0)
      - [AI overview effect still primary theory for decline](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=172.0)
  - [Tuning pages: Minor loss (\~10 sessions), not alarming](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=187.0)
  - [Treasury pages: Stable](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=223.0)
  - [Vendor pages: Significant increase in sessions/views across all pages](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=226.0)

### Content Production and Publishing

  - [Deadline for page creation extended to Friday](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
  - [Publishing 37 pages by next Friday (not Wednesday as initially requested)](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=335.0)
  - [Automation development starting next week to increase publishing capacity](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=365.0)
      - [Manual publishing currently a bottleneck](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=378.0)
      - [Automation expected to significantly scale output in 2-3 weeks](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=394.0)
  - [QA process for automated publishing:](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=424.0)
      - [Both human and automated checks planned](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=468.0)
      - [Weekly reports to provide visibility on potential issues](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=489.0)

### Blog Refresh Articles

  - [Addressed Matt's comments on initial batch](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=552.0)
  - [Retired one article due to content overlap](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=557.0)
  - [Working on H3 updates for Friday delivery](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=571.0)
  - [Tone adjustment needed:](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=629.0)
      - [Current drafts too conversational/"hokey" for Ramp's voice](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=629.0)
      - [Need to find balance between brand guidelines and appropriate tone](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=629.0)
  - [Formatting improvements required:](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=745.0)
      - [Break up "walls of text" with lists, tables, etc.](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=745.0)
      - [Enhance readability, especially on mobile](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=745.0)

## Next Steps

  - [GrowthX to deliver created pages by Friday for review](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
  - [Continue current publishing volume for next 2-3 weeks](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=405.0)
  - [Dev team to focus on designing publishing automation starting next week](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=365.0)
  - [Address tone and formatting issues in upcoming blog refresh articles](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=629.0)
  - [GrowthX to provide updates on vendor page automation progress](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=793.0)
  - [Implement QA process and reporting for automated publishing when ready](https://fathom.video/share/1iiP42M3MHBoBxn_o7-b96iANZVSFsyW?tab=summary&timestamp=468.0)

action_items.description: Publish 37 pages by Friday next week
Adjust tone in next batch of blog articles to be less conversational, align w/ RAMP's voice
Improve formatting in blog articles (break up text w/ lists, tables) for better readability, esp. on mobile
